title: Echinopsis hahniana
intro: Echinopsis hahniana, also known as the Lady of the Night or Queen of the Night, is a species of cactus native to Mexico that belongs to the Echinopsis genus. Traditionally used in medicine, Echinopsis hahniana is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Echinopsis hahniana is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea to take advantage of its medicinal benefits, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
taxonomy: Echinopsis hahniana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Echinopsis and the subgenus Echinopsis. The species is further categorized under the section Echinopsis and the tribe Cacteae. Within the family Cactaceae, Echinopsis hahniana is part of the subfamily Cactoideae. Its classification is also recognized in the APG III system, which is a widely accepted plant taxonomy system.
